title: đź”´ [BUG] SQL Injection vulnerability in /api/tasks/search endpoint
body:
## Description
The `/api/tasks/search` endpoint is vulnerable to SQL injection.
The keyword parameter is directly concatenated into SQL query string in `DatabaseHelper.java`.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Call `GET /api/tasks/search?keyword=' OR 1=1 --`
2. All tasks are returned regardless of search term

## Expected Behavior
The search should use parameterized queries and reject malicious input.

## Actual Behavior
The raw SQL query is executed, allowing data exfiltration.

## Impact
**CRITICAL** - Full database access possible through SQL injection.

## Files Affected
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/util/DatabaseHelper.java` (line 55-62)
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/service/TaskService.java` (searchTasks method)

title: đź”´ [BUG] Passwords stored in plaintext in User entity
body:
## Description
User passwords are stored in plaintext in the database. The `User` entity stores the raw password string and the `authenticate()` method compares plaintext values.

## Impact
**CRITICAL** - If the database is compromised, all user passwords are immediately exposed.

## Expected Behavior
Passwords should be hashed using bcrypt or similar before storage.

## Files Affected
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/model/User.java`
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/service/UserService.java`
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/config/DataInitializer.java`

Found by: Security audit 2023-Q4

title: đź”´ [BUG] ArithmeticException: Division by zero on empty project dashboard
body:
## Description
When a project has 0 tasks, the dashboard endpoint throws `ArithmeticException: / by zero`.

## Stack Trace
```
java.lang.ArithmeticException: / by zero
    at com.taskflow.model.Project.getProgress(Project.java:98)
    at com.taskflow.controller.ProjectController.getProjectDashboard(ProjectController.java:82)
```

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create a new project with no tasks
2. Call `GET /api/projects/{id}/dashboard`

## Environment
- Production
- Frequency: Every time for new projects

## Files Affected
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/model/Project.java` (getProgress method)

title: đź”´ [BUG] Intermittent date parsing errors under high load (thread-safety)
body:
## Description
In production, we see intermittent `NumberFormatException` and `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException` from `DateUtils.parseDate()`.

## Stack Trace
```
java.lang.NumberFormatException: For input string: ""
    at java.text.SimpleDateFormat.parse(SimpleDateFormat.java:...)
    at com.taskflow.util.DateUtils.parseDate(DateUtils.java:35)
```

## Root Cause (Suspected)
`DateUtils` uses static `SimpleDateFormat` instances which are NOT thread-safe. Under concurrent access (>100 requests), the internal state gets corrupted.

## Workaround
Restart the server when it happens.

## Files Affected
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/util/DateUtils.java` (static SimpleDateFormat fields)

title: đź”´ [BUG] NullPointerException in Task.toString() crashes batch jobs
body:
## Description
Getting NPE when logging tasks that don't have an assignee or due date.

## Stack Trace
```
java.lang.NullPointerException
    at com.taskflow.model.Task.toString(Task.java:98)
```

## Impact
This crashes several batch jobs that log task details.

## Files Affected
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/model/Task.java` (toString method)

title: đź”´ [BUG] getQuarter() returns 0-3 instead of 1-4 (off-by-one bug)
body:
## Description
The `DateUtils.getQuarter()` method returns quarter values 0-3 instead of the expected 1-4.

January returns 0 instead of 1, April returns 1 instead of 2, etc.

## Root Cause
`Calendar.MONTH` is 0-based (0=January). The calculation `month / 3` gives 0-3. It should be `(month / 3) + 1`.

## Test
`testGetQuarter` in `DateUtilsTest.java` was previously disabled due to this bug and is now enabled, causing CI failure.

## Files Affected
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/util/DateUtils.java` (getQuarter method, line ~128)

title: đźź  [BUG] StringUtils.padRight() throws StringIndexOutOfBoundsException
body:
## Description
`StringUtils.padRight()` throws `StringIndexOutOfBoundsException` when the input string is longer than the target length.

## Steps to Reproduce
```java
StringUtils.padRight("long string", 5); // throws exception
```

## Expected Behavior
Should return the original string (or truncate) when input is longer than target length.

## Files Affected
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/util/StringUtils.java` (padRight method)

title: đźź  [BUG] StringUtils.sanitize() is bypassable with encoded XSS payloads
body: ## Description The custom `sanitize()` method in StringUtils only removes `

title: đźźˇ [FEATURE] Need API pagination - OOM in production
body:
## Description
`GET /api/tasks` returns ALL tasks. We now have 50,000+ tasks and this endpoint causes `OutOfMemoryError` in production.

## Requirements
- Add pagination support (`page`, `size` parameters) on all list endpoints
- Default page size: 20
- Max page size: 100
- Return total count in response headers

## Impact
Production servers crash regularly when this endpoint is called.

## Files Affected
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/controller/TaskController.java`
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/service/TaskService.java`
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/repository/TaskRepository.java`

title: đźźˇ [FEATURE] Need proper authentication and authorization
body:
## Description
Currently there is no authentication middleware. Any request can access any endpoint. The login endpoint returns a fake JWT token.

## Requirements
- Proper JWT authentication with Spring Security
- Role-based access control (ADMIN, USER, VIEWER)
- Endpoint-level authorization
- Password hashing (related to issue #2)

## Priority
High - compliance audit coming in Q2

## Files Affected
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/controller/UserController.java`
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/config/WebConfig.java`
- Need new: `SecurityConfig.java`, `JwtFilter.java`

title: đźźˇ [FEATURE] Upgrade Spring Boot from 2.7 to 3.x
body:
## Description
Spring Boot 2.7.x reached End of Life. We need to upgrade to Spring Boot 3.x.

## Migration Requirements
- Migrate from `javax.*` to `jakarta.*` namespace
- Update Java version requirement to 17+
- Review all deprecated API usage
- Update Spring Security configuration (if added)
- Test all endpoints after migration

## Current State
- Spring Boot: 2.7.18 (EOL)
- Java: 11 (needs upgrade to 17+)

## Files Affected
- `pom.xml`
- All Java files using `javax.*` imports

title: đźźˇ [FEATURE] Add API documentation with Swagger/OpenAPI
body:
## Description
There is no API documentation. Developers rely on reading source code to understand endpoints.

## Requirements
- Add SpringDoc OpenAPI (swagger-ui)
- Document all REST endpoints
- Include request/response examples
- Add API versioning information

## Acceptance Criteria
- Swagger UI available at `/swagger-ui.html`
- All endpoints documented with descriptions
- Request/response schemas visible

title: đźźˇ [FEATURE] Add input validation on all API endpoints
body:
## Description
No input validation exists on any API endpoint. Tasks can be created with null titles, negative priorities, and invalid status codes.

## Requirements
- Add Bean Validation annotations (`@NotNull`, `@Size`, `@Min`, `@Max`)
- Add `@Valid` on controller method parameters
- Return proper 400 Bad Request responses with validation error details
- Validate status transitions
- Validate priority range (1-5)

## Files Affected
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/model/Task.java`
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/model/User.java`
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/model/Project.java`
- All controllers

title: đźźˇ [FEATURE] Add proper error handling and consistent HTTP status codes
body:
## Description
Error handling is inconsistent across the API. Some errors return 500 with stack traces, others return null, and some throw uncaught exceptions.

## Requirements
- Add `@ControllerAdvice` for global exception handling
- Return proper HTTP status codes (400, 404, 409, 422, 500)
- Consistent error response format (code, message, details)
- Never expose stack traces in production
- Log all errors with correlation IDs

## Files Affected
- Need new: `GlobalExceptionHandler.java`
- All controllers need review

title: đźźˇ [FEATURE] Add database migration tool (Flyway/Liquibase)
body:
## Description
Database schema changes are managed by Hibernate `ddl-auto=update`, which is dangerous in production. No migration history, no rollback capability.

## Requirements
- Add Flyway or Liquibase for database migration management
- Create initial migration from current schema
- Set `ddl-auto=validate` in production
- Migration scripts for all future schema changes

## Impact
Without this, any schema change risks data loss in production.

title: đźź  [TECH DEBT] TaskService is a God Class (670+ lines) - needs refactoring
body:
## Description
`TaskService.java` is a God Class with 670+ lines handling 8+ responsibilities:
- CRUD operations
- Task assignment
- Status workflow/transitions
- Notifications (inline, not using NotificationService)
- Statistics/reporting
- Import/export (CSV)
- Sprint management
- Search

## Impact
- Impossible to write proper unit tests
- Hard to understand the code
- Every change risks breaking something else
- High cyclomatic complexity

## Recommendation
Break into separate services:
- `TaskCrudService`
- `TaskWorkflowService`
- `TaskNotificationService`
- `TaskReportingService`
- `TaskImportExportService`

## Files Affected
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/service/TaskService.java`

title: đźź  [TECH DEBT] Replace custom StringUtils with Apache Commons Lang
body:
## Description
We have a custom `StringUtils` class that duplicates functionality already available in Apache Commons Lang 3 (which is already in our dependencies!).

Our version has known bugs:
- `sanitize()` is trivially bypassable (see issue #8)
- `padRight()` throws StringIndexOutOfBoundsException (see issue #7)
- `toSnakeCase()` doesn't handle consecutive uppercase letters

## Recommendation
Delete our custom version and use `org.apache.commons.lang3.StringUtils` directly.

## Files Affected
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/util/StringUtils.java` (to be removed)
- All files importing our custom StringUtils

title: đź”´ [TECH DEBT] Remove hardcoded credentials from source code
body:
## Description
Found hardcoded credentials in multiple files:

| File | Type | Detail |
|------|------|--------|
| `application.properties` | DB password | Commented-out production password (never rotated!) |
| `DatabaseHelper.java` | DB credentials | Static fields with username/password |
| `NotificationService.java` | SMTP password | Hardcoded email credentials |
| `NotificationService.java` | Slack webhook | Hardcoded webhook URL |
| `DataInitializer.java` | User passwords | Plaintext passwords for seed data |
| `application.properties` | JWT secret | Hardcoded signing key |

## Impact
**CRITICAL** - Credentials in source code are a major security risk. Production DB password was accidentally committed and never rotated.

## Recommendation
Move all secrets to environment variables or a secrets manager (e.g., AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault).

title: đźź  [TECH DEBT] DatabaseHelper uses raw JDBC - should use Spring Data JPA
body:
## Description
`DatabaseHelper.java` contains raw JDBC code that:
1. Concatenates user input directly into SQL strings (SQL injection)
2. Never closes ResultSet, Statement, or Connection objects (resource leaks)
3. Provides an `executeQuery()` method that runs arbitrary SQL
4. Uses thread-unsafe singleton pattern for connections

## Recommendation
This class should be completely removed and replaced with Spring Data JPA repositories (which already exist in the project).

## Files Affected
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/util/DatabaseHelper.java` (to be removed)
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/service/TaskService.java` (uses DatabaseHelper)

title: đźź  [TECH DEBT] Test suite is unreliable - disabled tests, no assertions, no cleanup
body:
## Description
Our test suite has multiple problems:

- **7 tests are `@Disabled`** because they were 'flaky' or 'TODO'
- **Several tests have no assertions** (inflate coverage numbers)
- **No `@BeforeEach` cleanup** - tests interfere with each other
- **Depend on DataInitializer data** - fragile and breaks when seed data changes
- **0% coverage on controllers**
- **0% coverage on security-critical code paths**
- Estimated effective test coverage: **<10%**

## Requirements
- Fix or properly implement all disabled tests
- Add meaningful assertions to assertion-less tests
- Add `@BeforeEach` cleanup to prevent test interference
- Add controller tests (integration tests)
- Add security-related test cases
- Target: >60% meaningful test coverage

## Files Affected
- `src/test/java/com/taskflow/service/TaskServiceTest.java`
- `src/test/java/com/taskflow/util/DateUtilsTest.java`
- `src/test/java/com/taskflow/util/StringUtilsTest.java`

title: đźź  [TECH DEBT] Inconsistent naming conventions throughout codebase
body:
## Description
The codebase mixes naming conventions inconsistently:

| Convention | Examples |
|-----------|----------|
| camelCase | `reporterId`, `projectCode`, `createdDate` |
| snake_case | `assignee_id`, `due_date`, `created_at`, `phone_number` |
| Mixed fields | `full_name` vs `firstName`/`lastName` |
| Mixed getters | `getOwner_id()` vs `getCode()` |

## Impact
- Code is confusing to read
- API responses have inconsistent field names
- New developers make wrong assumptions

## Recommendation
Standardize on Java camelCase convention for all fields and methods. Use `@Column(name=...)` for database mapping where needed.

title: đźź  [TECH DEBT] Dead code and deprecated methods should be removed
body:
## Description
Found several pieces of dead code:

- `TaskService.getOldDashboardStats()` - deprecated, referenced by decommissioned admin panel
- `TaskService.migrateLegacyIds()` - was for JIRA migration in 2019
- `TaskService.generateWeeklyReport()` - replaced by dashboard
- `Task.workflow_state` field - from old workflow engine
- `Task.legacy_id` field - from JIRA migration
- `Project.color`/`icon` fields - from old dashboard

## Impact
Dead code adds confusion and maintenance burden. New developers waste time understanding unused code.

## Files Affected
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/service/TaskService.java`
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/model/Task.java`
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/model/Project.java`

title: đźź  [TECH DEBT] CORS is wide open - needs proper configuration
body:
## Description
`WebConfig.java` has CORS configured to allow ALL origins (`*`), ALL methods, and ALL headers. This was a 'temporary fix' that was never addressed.

```java
// FIXME: This is too permissive
registry.addMapping("/**")
        .allowedOrigins("*")
        .allowedMethods("*")
        .allowedHeaders("*");
```

## Impact
Any website can make API calls to our backend, enabling CSRF attacks and data theft.

## Recommendation
Restrict CORS to specific allowed origins (frontend domain), specific HTTP methods, and required headers.

## Files Affected
- `src/main/java/com/taskflow/config/WebConfig.java`

title: đźź  [TECH DEBT] Jackson version conflict with Spring Boot parent
body:
## Description
The `pom.xml` explicitly declares Jackson Databind 2.13.0, which conflicts with the version managed by Spring Boot 2.7.18 parent POM.

```xml
<!-- FIXME: jackson version conflict with spring boot parent -->
<dependency>
    <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
    <artifactId>jackson-databind</artifactId>
    <version>2.13.0</version>
</dependency>
```

## Impact
- Potential runtime `NoSuchMethodError` or `ClassNotFoundException`
- Spring Boot's Jackson auto-configuration may not work correctly
- Security patches from Spring Boot parent POM are overridden

## Recommendation
Remove the explicit version and let Spring Boot manage the Jackson version.

## Files Affected
- `pom.xml`

title: đźź  [TECH DEBT] Thread-unsafe singleton pattern in DatabaseHelper
đźźˇ [DEVOPS] Add code coverage reporting (JaCoCo)
body:
## Description
There is no code coverage reporting configured. We have no visibility into which code is tested and which is not.

## Requirements
- Add JaCoCo Maven plugin
- Generate coverage reports on CI builds
- Set minimum coverage thresholds (start with 30%, increase over time)
- Upload coverage reports as CI artifacts
- Consider adding Codecov or similar for PR coverage checks

## Files Affected
- `pom.xml` (add JaCoCo plugin)
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (add coverage step)

title: đźźˇ [DEVOPS] Add static code analysis (SpotBugs/Checkstyle)
body:
## Description
No static code analysis tools are configured. Bugs, style violations, and potential issues are only caught during code review (if at all).

## Requirements
- Add SpotBugs Maven plugin for bug detection
- Add Checkstyle for code style enforcement
- Configure rules appropriate for the project
- Run as part of CI pipeline
- Fail build on critical findings

## Files Affected
- `pom.xml`
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- New: checkstyle config file

title: đźź  [DEVOPS] mysql-connector-java dependency included but unused
body:
## Description
The `pom.xml` includes `mysql-connector-java` (8.0.28) but the application uses H2 in-memory database. MySQL is not configured or used.

```xml
<!-- TODO: someone added mysql but we never use it in prod -->
<dependency>
    <groupId>mysql</groupId>
    <artifactId>mysql-connector-java</artifactId>
    <version>8.0.28</version>
</dependency>
```

## Impact
- Unnecessary dependency increases build size
- Potential security risk from unused library
- Confusing for developers

## Recommendation
Remove the MySQL dependency. Add it back when/if MySQL support is actually needed, with a proper database profile.

## Files Affected
- `pom.xml`

title: đź’¬ [DISCUSSION] Should we move to microservices?
body:
## Context
With all the coupling issues in our monolith, should we consider breaking this into microservices?

## Arguments For
- TaskService is too coupled to everything
- Scaling issues with the monolith
- Team velocity is decreasing
- Independent deployment of components

## Arguments Against
- We're a small team (3 people)
- Added operational complexity (K8s, service mesh, distributed tracing)
- Migration effort is significant
- Risk of distributed monolith

## Questions
1. What would the service boundaries be?
2. Do we have the infrastructure knowledge?
3. What's the migration timeline?

Looking for discussion before we decide.

cc: @kevin @jennifer @david

title: đź’¬ [DISCUSSION] Log4j 1.x still in use - security risk assessment needed
body:
## Context
We're still using Log4j 1.x (version 1.2.17) which has been EOL since 2015.

While this is **NOT** the same as the Log4Shell vulnerability (CVE-2021-44228, which affected Log4j 2.x), it still has known CVEs and receives no security patches:
- CVE-2019-17571 (deserialization in SocketServer)
- CVE-2022-23302 (JMSSink)
- CVE-2022-23305 (JDBCAppender SQL injection)
- CVE-2022-23307 (Chainsaw deserialization)

## Questions
1. Should we prioritize migrating to Logback/SLF4J?
2. Are we using any of the vulnerable components?
3. What's the migration effort?

This was supposed to be done in 2022 (see task in backlog).

## Files Affected
- `pom.xml` (log4j dependency)
- All files using log4j imports
